goal: Add SteeringQueue/v1 as a deterministic runtime cockpit packet that records queued human steering, continuation prerequisites, stale-state checks, and applied/rejected outcomes before autonomous continuation relies on prior instructions.

forbiddenFiles: agent scheduler or background worker implementation
raw prompt transcripts, raw steering text, secrets, token values, or credential material
broad rewrite of runtime-card, delivery-truth, or pr-closeout command families

nonGoals: Do not build the continuation runner, thread scheduler, or background queue executor in this slice.
Do not auto-apply queued steering; this packet classifies whether steering is eligible, stale, expired, rejected, superseded, or already applied.
Do not treat a steering queue item as claim-supporting delivery evidence.
Do not store raw user prompts, transcripts, secrets, or token values in the queue.
Do not mutate Codex source, Linear, GitHub, or external tracker state.
implementationApproach: Define SteeringQueue/v1 under a new deep module, src/lib/steering-queue, rather than embedding the contract inside delivery-truth or runtime-card.

Model each queue item with stable id, createdAt, expiresAt, sourceRef, instructionRef, instructionHash, instructionHashAlgorithm, instructionCanonicalizationVersion, instructionProvenanceKind, deliveryMode, expected thread/turn/head/artifact prerequisites, artifact identity refs, supersession refs, state, state timestamps, and stale precondition evidence.
Support explicit item states: pending, applicable, applied, rejected, expired, superseded, and stale.

Derive an item evaluation from current thread id, turn id, head SHA, artifact identity refs, instruction hash verification, and an explicit evaluator-supplied nowIso timestamp; do not accept caller-supplied eligibility as authoritative.

Classify stale preconditions for stale turn, stale head, instruction hash mismatch, instruction hash unverifiable, artifact identity mismatch, superseded artifact, expired queue, missing artifact, rejected steering, and already applied steering.

Define artifact prerequisites as immutable identities that include artifactRef, headSha, producedAt, and sha256 digest or receipt id; a same-path artifact with a different digest must evaluate as stale.
Use strict allowlist schemas with additionalProperties=false at every object level and bounded free-text fields, rather than relying only on sensitive-key denylists.

Define a deterministic item selection order for multiple eligible items in the same scope: supersession lineage first, explicit priority second, createdAt descending third, and id ascending as the final tie-breaker.
Expose a packet summary that counts pending, applicable, stale, expired, superseded, rejected, and applied items without embedding raw steering text.

Publish JSON Schema, example, manifest entry, TypeScript builder/evaluator, a narrow script-backed validator, and fixtures for applicable happy path, stale turn, stale head, instruction hash mismatch, hash unverifiable, same-path artifact digest mismatch, superseded artifact, expired queue, duplicate item id, rejected steering, applied steering, and conflict-precedence ordering.
Keep the packet as orientation and audit-trail evidence only; later runtime-card or continuation wiring may reference it but this slice does not give it execution authority.
implementationInvariants: SteeringQueue/v1 must be additive and not change existing runtime-card, delivery-truth, pr-closeout, or harness next behavior.

Instruction identity verification uses sha256 over canonical UTF-8 LF-normalized instruction-source bytes when the source is locally available; when the source cannot be read or recomputed, the item must classify as stale with instruction_hash_unverifiable rather than applicable.

Allowed instructionProvenanceKind values must be bounded to durable artifact classes such as harness_artifact, runtime_card, review_artifact, goal_artifact, or external_snapshot_ref; free-form conversational memory is not a trusted provenance source.
Queue items cannot be applicable when expected thread id, turn id, head SHA, or required artifact refs do not match the current evaluation context.
Queue items cannot be applicable when required artifact identity refs are missing, digest/head/producedAt values differ, or a required artifact has been superseded.
Expired items cannot be applicable, even if all other prerequisites match.
Rejected and applied items cannot be re-applied by eligibility evaluation.
Superseded items cannot be applicable when their supersededBy reference is present or a required artifact has been superseded.
Applied items must include appliedAt and must not include unresolved stale preconditions.
Rejected items must include rejectedAt and a non-empty stateReason.
Terminal states are immutable for eligibility: applied, rejected, expired, superseded, and stale cannot transition back to pending or applicable in the same packet.
State timestamps must be monotonic: createdAt <= staleAt/rejectedAt/appliedAt/supersededAt/expiredAt when those timestamps are present, and expiresAt must be later than createdAt.

Conflicting terminal signals collapse deterministically by precedence: rejected > applied > superseded > expired > stale > applicable > pending; the evaluator must report the lower-precedence conflicting signals as stalePreconditions.

When multiple items are applicable for the same continuation scope, selectedItemId must be deterministic using supersession lineage, explicit priority, createdAt descending, and id ascending tie-breaking.
Item ids, refs, and hashes must be non-empty safe strings; ids must be unique within the packet.
The schema and semantic validator must reject raw prompt, transcript, secret, token, credential, and rawSteering fields at every object level.

Agent discoverability is a follow-on wiring requirement: this slice must publish the packet and validator contract in the runtime-packet manifest, but it must not claim runtime-card or harness next discoverability until a later slice projects queue summaries into those surfaces.
riskControls: Keep steering content indirect through instructionRef and instructionHash so the packet can prove identity without copying sensitive text.
Bind continuation eligibility to thread, turn, head SHA, artifact identities, verified instruction hash, and expiry instead of conversational memory.
Separate queue orientation from claim support so stale steering cannot make delivery claims pass.
Use an evaluator-supplied nowIso timestamp for expiry classification so tests and agents can reproduce verdicts exactly.
Use deterministic fixtures for each stale-state class before exposing the packet to runtime-card or continuation workflows.
Keep the validator script read-only and artifact-local; it must validate a JSON packet and exit non-zero on schema or semantic failures.
validationPlan: pnpm exec biome check src/lib/steering-queue/index.ts src/lib/steering-queue/steering-queue.ts src/lib/steering-queue/steering-queue.test.ts contracts/steering-queue.schema.json contracts/examples/steering-queue.example.json contracts/runtime-packet-schemas.manifest.json scripts/validate-steering-queue.cjs src/dev/validate-runtime-packet-schemas-script.test.ts
